phases.name: Phase 1 — CLI contract and security policy
phases.goal: Define macOS-specific authentication requirements for `secret get` and `inject` while preserving current interactive-only behavior on Linux.

tasks.title: Finalize command contract for macOS-authenticated secret get and inject
outputs: Final command contract for `secret get` on macOS and Linux
Final command contract for `inject` on macOS and Linux
Documented rule that macOS requires both `_is_interactive()` and device owner authentication
Documented rule that Linux keeps `_is_interactive()`-only behavior

tasks.title: Define authentication and failure UX for secret get and inject
outputs: Failure message matrix for unsupported platform, auth unavailable, auth cancelled, helper missing, and Keychain access failure
User-facing guidance for `secret get` auth failures
User-facing guidance for `inject` auth failures

tasks.title: Document phase 1 decisions and acceptance criteria
outputs: Documented phase summary
Acceptance checklist for macOS auth on `secret get` and `inject`
Implementation handoff notes

phases.name: Phase 2 — Native helper implementation
phases.goal: Implement a small Swift helper that performs macOS device owner authentication and authenticated Keychain reads for `secret get` and `inject`.

tasks.title: Establish Swift helper layout and build approach
outputs: Chosen source location for helper
Build instructions for local development
Expected runtime path contract for Python integration

tasks.title: Implement LocalAuthentication device owner auth flow
outputs: Swift authentication flow using `LAContext`
Authentication availability checks
Authentication cancel and failure handling

tasks.title: Implement authenticated Keychain read flow in helper
inputs: Requirement to avoid secret leakage in errors
outputs: Swift Keychain query using Security framework
Authentication-context-aware secret retrieval
Sanitized stdout/stderr contract

tasks.title: Define helper exit codes and invocation contract
outputs: Documented exit code mapping
Stable CLI arguments for helper
Invocation examples for integration and testing

phases.name: Phase 3 — Python backend and CLI integration
phases.goal: Integrate the native helper into the macOS backend so that `secret get` and `inject` require both `_is_interactive()` and device owner authentication on macOS, while Linux remains `_is_interactive()`-only.

tasks.title: Add authenticated retrieval path to macOS backend
outputs: Backend helper invocation wrapper
Authenticated retrieval method for macOS secret access
Sanitized error mapping into `EnvrcctlError`

tasks.title: Update secret get to require interactive shell and macOS auth
outputs: Updated `secret get` behavior on macOS
macOS enforcement of `_is_interactive()` plus device owner authentication
Preserved Linux `_is_interactive()`-only behavior

tasks.title: Update inject to require interactive shell and macOS auth
outputs: Updated `inject` behavior on macOS
macOS enforcement of `_is_interactive()` plus device owner authentication
Preserved Linux `_is_interactive()` plus `--force` behavior

tasks.title: Preserve fail-closed behavior for unsupported auth states
outputs: Unsupported platform error handling
Missing helper failure handling
Fail-closed behavior for auth cancellation and auth-unavailable states

phases.name: Phase 4 — Tests
phases.goal: Cover the new authenticated `secret get` and `inject` flow with unit, mocked integration, and manual macOS verification checks.

tasks.title: Add CLI tests for secret get authentication behavior
outputs: CLI tests for interactive vs non-interactive behavior
CLI tests for macOS auth-required behavior
CLI tests for Linux interactive-only behavior

tasks.title: Add CLI tests for inject authentication behavior
outputs: CLI tests for interactive vs non-interactive behavior
CLI tests for macOS auth-required behavior
CLI tests for Linux `--force` behavior

tasks.title: Add backend tests for helper invocation and error mapping
outputs: Unit tests for helper command construction
Tests for stdout/stderr handling
Tests for non-zero exit code mapping

inputs: Success and failure cases for helper integration
outputs: Mocked success-path tests
Mocked auth-cancel tests

Mocked auth-unavailable tests

outputs: Manual checklist for Touch ID verification
Manual checklist for auth cancel and unavailable flows
Manual checklist for Apple Watch-assisted approval where offered by macOS

phases.name: Phase 5 — Documentation and rollout
phases.goal: Document macOS authentication requirements for `secret get` and `inject`, while clarifying Linux remains `_is_interactive()`-guarded.

tasks.title: Update README for macOS-authenticated secret get and inject
outputs: Updated README sections for `secret get` behavior
